content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 21, 1990


Steve Pedrazzi
President
Board of Directors
Patterson Hospital District
Del Puerto Hospital
P. O. Box 187
Patterson, CA  95363

                                          Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
                                               Our File No. I-90-137

Dear Mr. Pedrazzi:

     This letter is in response to your request for advice regard-
ing the duties and responsibilities of Mr. Walter Rea, Sr., under
the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").  You have not stated in your letter that Mr. Rea has
authorized your request for advice.  Accordingly, we consider your
letter to be a request for informal assistance pursuant to Regula-
tion 18329(c) (copy enclosed.)²  We do not provide advice concern-
ing laws other than the Act.  Please consult with the Office of
the California Attorney General regarding the doctrine of
incompatible public offices.

                              QUESTIONS

     1.  Do the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act
disqualify Mr. Rea from participating in decisions regarding
certain real property presently owned by the Patterson Hospital
District?

     2.  Do the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act
disqualify Mr. Rea from speaking publicly on issues related to the
disposition or use of the real property in question?


_________________________
     ¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

     ² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.  (Sec-
tion 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

File No. I-90-137
Page 2


     3.  Are the offices of hospital district board member and
president of a county commission on aging incompatible offices?

                            CONCLUSIONS

     1.  The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act do not
disqualify Mr. Rea from participating in decisions regarding real
property owned by the district.

     2.  The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act do not
disqualify Mr. Rea from speaking publicly on issues related to the
disposition or use of the real property in question.

     3.  We do not provide advice concerning laws other than the
Act.  Please consult with the Office of the California Attorney
General regarding the doctrine of incompatible public offices.

                               FACTS

     You are the President of the Board of Directors of the
Paterson Hospital District (the district).  In that capacity, it
is your duty to advise other members of the board concerning their
duties and responsibilities under the Act.  You seek our advice to
determine whether Mr. Walter Rea, Sr., may participate in certain
decisions related to a parcel of real property owned by the
district.

     Hospital districts are empowered to hold property, exercise
eminent domain, operate health facilities, and do any and all acts
to carry out the local hospital district law.  (Health and Safety
Code Section 32121.)  The purpose of the local hospital district
law is to protect the public health and welfare by furnishing
hospital facilities and health care services.

     Mr. Rea is a member of the board of the district.  He is also
President of the Stanislaus County Commission on Aging.³  The du-
ties and powers of local commissioners on aging are set forth in
Welfare and Institutions Code Section 9351.


___
3    For the purposes of the Act, income does not include:

          salary and reimbursement for expenses or per
     diem received from a state, local, or federal
     government agency and reimbursement for travel
     expenses and per diem received from a bona fide
     educational, academic, or charitable organization.

                            Section 82030(b)(2).

     Thus, income received from the County Commission on Aging, if any,
does not create a disqualifying financial interest.

File No. I-90-137
Page 3


The district owns a parcel of real property adjacent to the
district's hospital facilities. The district sought to develop a
medical office complex on the parcel, and applied to the City of
Patterson for zoning approval. The city denied approval.

During the public meeting of the city council at which the
project was considered, Mr. Rea, who opposed the project, offered
to buy the parcel for $5.00 more than the best offer the hospital
might receive. He further offered to donate the real property to
the city provided the city would commit to the use of the subject
property for a senior citizen center. Attached to your request
for advice is a transcript of Mr. Rea's statements at the city
council meeting.

Local efforts are currently under way by private citizens to
overturn the city's refusal to approve the project through
circulation of a ballot title and summary pursuant to Elections
Code Sections 4001-4011. In response to these efforts, Mr. Rea
has continued to speak out publicly against the district's
proposed project. Pending the outcome of the public petition,
the district may be required to consider further action related to
the project.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making,
participating in, or using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official knows or has
reason to know he or she has a financial interest. Members of the
board of a hospital district are public officials. (Section
82048.)

An official has a financial interest in a decision if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally, on the official or a member of his or her immediate
family or on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to, received

File No. I-90-137
Page 4

by or promised to the public official within 12
months prior to the time when the decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent
for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.

Section 87103(a)-(e).

You have not indicated in your request for advice whether
Mr. Rea has a financial interest which might disqualify him from
participating in decisions regarding the hospital district. You
have only indicated that Mr. Rea offered to purchase a parcel of
land owned by the hospital district. It appears from your letter
that Mr. Rea did not purchase the property. You also indicate
that Mr. Rea has expressed his opposition to the proposed medical
office complex. These facts are not disqualifying. Absent a
financial interest, Mr. Rea may participate freely in all matters
concerning the district, including decisions regarding the real
property at issue.

Should you have any further questions regarding this matter,
do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:BMB:plh

Enclosure
